etymology.description: 肉(肉) (ròu, 'meat') corrupted to 夕(夕) (xī) -> duplicated 夕(夕) = many
etymology.interpretation: Originally depicted two pieces of meat 肉(肉) (ròu, 'meat'), suggesting abundance or 'many'. Over time, 肉(肉) was corrupted to 夕(夕) (xī), and the character became a duplication of 夕(夕), signifying multiplicity or 'many'. This aligns with other duplicative characters like 林(林) (lín, 'forest').

examples.chinese: 书架上有很多书(書架上有很多書)。
examples.pinyin: Shūjià shàng yǒu hěn duō shū.
examples.english: There are many books on the shelf.

examples.chinese: 他工作得比我多(他工作得比我多)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gōngzuò de bǐ wǒ duō.
examples.english: He works more than I do.

examples.chinese: 多喝水有益健康(多喝水有益健康)。
examples.english: Drinking more water is beneficial to health.
